label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question: "___ made Woolworth a world-famous man." The options are A through D, with each describing a different aspect of Woolworth's life or business practices. Let me go step by step through the text to find the answer. The story describes Woolworth's early life, his decision not to be a farmer, taking a business course, and becoming a salesman. He had a natural skill for displaying goods, which led to a successful experiment by selling items for five cents each. This led to his own store. The key point the text emphasizes is his business approach of selling low-priced goods. More importantly, he learned to buy in large quantities directly from factories to keep prices low. An example given is his large order of knives from Germany which cut the price in half. By 1919, he had many stores in the US, Canada, and London, and he became very wealthy and famous. The text ends by stating he always ran his business according to strict rules, the most important being "THE CUSTOMER IS ALWAYS RIGHT." The question is asking for the reason why Woolworth became a world-famous man. The text clearly says his business practices (selling low-priced goods, buying in bulk directly from factories, and his strict business rules) led to his success and fame. His wealth (millions) and his name becoming famous throughout the world are directly mentioned. Looking at the answer choices: A. His business skills and his wealth B. The low price of the goods he sold C. His trip to Germany and his huge order of knives D. His natural skill for displaying things Option A combines business skills (which the text describes in several ways, including his approach to low pricing and buying in bulk) and his wealth (which the text clearly states made him famous). The text says, "He made many millions and his name became famous throughout the world." So, it's the combination of his business acumen and the resulting wealth that made him famous. Option B is partially correct, as the low price of goods was a key part of his business model, but it's just one aspect of his business skills, not the complete answer.
